---
title: "Resolution bugs after playing Enemy Territory"
date: 2008-03-04
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by pipesnipe on 2008-03-04
After playing enemy territory, my screen resolution is lower than normal. I have to push the screen around with the mouse...

I think there is something wrong with my xorg configuration. I Tried dual-desktop once, and after that, i have done a lot of testing of the "Screens and graphics" manager...

> # xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

### Post by IdanH14 on 2008-03-09
I have the exact same problem. I went through a lot of posts here concerning this problem, and none of them solved this problem completely, but rather had all kinds of work-arounds. 
This post says it all:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1280439&postcount=3](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1280439&postcount=3)
Hopefully someone will find a solution for this problem. In the meantime, you can restart X-Windows after exiting the game (CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE).

---
